description: Transparency-log witness-cosignature golden vector (RFC-ACDP-0015 §4–§5, §8). A single independent witness observes the log-001 golden checkpoint (tree_size 5, root_hash sha256:0b5978..., the same checkpoint pinned by log-001/log-003), verifies its signature and consistency, and cosigns it with the witness's OWN Ed25519 assertionMethod key (a DISTINCT witness test keypair, seed 0x33 repeated 32x — NOT the registry receipt key). Pins the JCS cosignature preimage (object minus 'signature'), the cosignature hash, the signature input (ASCII 'sha256:<hex>' string), and the Ed25519 signature — the RFC-ACDP-0010 §5 construction verbatim, keyed by the witness. This is the sig-001-equivalent for the witness layer; the vectors chain to log-001 (chains_to_log_fixture). Executed arithmetically by scripts/conformance-runner.py. Test keypair MUST NOT be used in production. Values generated by temp/gen-0.4.0-vectors.py.

witness_test_keypair.warning: TEST ONLY. The private seed is publicly known (32 bytes of 0x33). Never use this keypair for real cosignatures.

vectors.name: Witness cosigns the log-001 tree-size-5 checkpoint
verification_steps: 1. JCS-canonicalize cosignature_unsigned; result MUST equal expected.canonical_form byte-for-byte (RFC-ACDP-0015 §4–§5).
2. cosignature_hash = 'sha256:' + SHA-256 hex of the canonical bytes; MUST equal expected.cosignature_hash.

3. Sign the ASCII bytes of expected.cosignature_hash with the witness test private key; base64 MUST equal expected.signature_value_base64; verification with the witness test public key MUST succeed (RFC-ACDP-0015 §5 — the RFC-ACDP-0010 §5 construction, keyed by the witness).
4. Witness binding: the DID portion of signature.key_id MUST equal witness_id (RFC-ACDP-0015 §8 step 3).
5. Chain: witnessed_checkpoint.{log_id,tree_size,root_hash} MUST equal the log-001 golden checkpoint (the thing being cosigned).
6. cosignature_version MUST be 'acdp-cosig/1'; witnessed_at and witnessed_checkpoint.timestamp MUST be canonical millisecond RFC 3339 UTC.
7. This checkpoint is 1-witnessed for a consumer trusting did:web:witness.example.org (RFC-ACDP-0015 §8).
